query:
Research the company "jmsmucker".

Research the company's: (1) Recent financial performance trends, specifically looking for gaps between functional KPIs being hit vs. overall P&L misses or margin pressure, (2) Recent CEO changes or executive leadership transitions, (3) Current strategic initiatives including new product platform launches or market expansions, (4) Recent earnings misses or guidance reductions (especially 2026 guidance), (5) Production/manufacturing scale and recent capacity changes, (6) Pricing strategy changes or promotional effectiveness data, (7) Distribution network expansion or changes, (8) Recent cost-cutting initiatives that executed on plan but didn't move the needle on bottom-line results. Focus on identifying companies with structural gaps where individual functions are optimizing independently.

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
**Executive Summary**
The J.M. Smucker Co. is currently navigating a period of significant structural transition, marked by a comprehensive C-suite reorganization in early 2026 and a major realignment of its manufacturing footprint. While the company is aggressively investing in high-growth platforms like Uncrustables and integrating its Hostess Brands acquisition, it faces notable macroeconomic headwinds, including tariff pressures and cautious consumer spending, which have led to a Q2 2026 revenue miss and forecasted sales declines for fiscal 2027. 

**Financial Performance & Guidance Reductions**
Recent financial disclosures reveal a structural gap where functional segment wins are being offset by broader macroeconomic and margin pressures:
*   **Earnings Miss & Guidance Cuts:** In Q2 2026, J.M. Smucker reported a revenue miss with sales of $2.3 billion, representing a 0.86% negative surprise, though it met analyst EPS expectations of $2.10 [Earnings call transcript: JM Smucker Q2 2026 sees steady EPS, stock dips By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-jm-smucker-q2-2026-sees-steady-eps-stock-dips-93CH-4377175). The company's full-year fiscal 2026 guidance midpoint projects 3% growth, a sharp deceleration from the 7% growth seen in the prior year [FinancialContent - Why J. M. Smucker (SJM) Stock Is Up Today](https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/article/stockstory-2026-6-9-why-j-m-smucker-sjm-stock-is-up-today). Looking further ahead to fiscal 2027, the company expects net sales to decrease by 3.0% to 4.0% [The J.M. Smucker Co. Announces Fiscal Year 2026 Fourth Quarter Results and Provides Full-Year Fiscal 2027 Outlook](https://www.barchart.com/story/news/2373894/the-j-m-smucker-co-announces-fiscal-year-2026-fourth-quarter-results-and-provides-full-year-fiscal-2027-outlook).
*   **KPIs vs. Margin Pressures:** Management noted that while the coffee segment achieved positive elasticity for fiscal 2026, this functional win is being entirely offset by higher tariffs and updated hedging impacts, with tariffs embedding a $0.50 per share cost impact into FY26 guidance [Smucker (SJM) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript @themotleyfool #stocks $SJM](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2025/08/27/smucker-sjm-q1-2026-earnings-call-transcript/); [Smucker (SJM) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript - The Globe and Mail](https://www.theglobeandmail.com/investing/markets/markets-news/Motley%20Fool/34424078/smucker-sjm-q1-2026-earnings-call-transcript/). 
*   **Pricing Strategy:** In Q4 2026, the company relied on price increases to offset underlying volume declines in its spreads and coffee segments [Why J.M. Smucker Stock Jumped Today](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/why-j-m-smucker-stock-021034953.html). 
*   **Cash Flow Bright Spot:** Despite top-line pressures, J.M. Smucker raised its full-year fiscal 2026 free cash flow guidance by $100 million (to $975 million), driven by ongoing annual legislative benefits [Smucker (SJM) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript - The Globe and Mail](https://www.theglobeandmail.com/investing/markets/markets-news/Motley%20Fool/34424078/smucker-sjm-q1-2026-earnings-call-transcript/).

**Executive Leadership Transitions (Last 6 Months)**
The company executed a massive leadership restructuring in early 2026, fundamentally altering its operational hierarchy:
*   **C-Suite Reorganization:** Effective February 9, 2026, the company eliminated the Chief Operating Officer (COO) role, resulting in the departure of President and COO John Brase [Smucker (NYSE: SJM) reshapes senior team and affirms 2026 outlook](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/SJM/8-k-j-m-smucker-co-reports-material-event-5a3d07d58f0e.html). Mark Smucker reassumed the title of President, now serving as CEO, President, and Chair of the Board [The J.M. Smucker Co. Announces Senior Leadership Updates to Drive Continued Growth and Execution](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/the-jm-smucker-co-announces-senior-leadership-updates-to-drive-continued-growth-and-execution-302683731.html).
*   **Promotions & Expanded Roles:** Tucker Marshall was promoted to CFO and EVP of Frozen Handheld, Spreads, and Sweet Baked Snacks, adding sales oversight to his purview. Rob Ferguson was named Chief Product Supply Officer and EVP of Coffee, Pet, and Away From Home. Jill Penrose was promoted to Chief People and Administrative Officer and Chief of Staff [Smucker (NYSE: SJM) reshapes senior team and affirms 2026 outlook](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/SJM/8-k-j-m-smucker-co-reports-material-event-5a3d07d58f0e.html).
*   **Marketing & Sales Shifts:** Katie Williams (formerly of Haleon) was elected Chief Marketing Officer effective February 5, 2026, replacing retiring CMO Gail Hollander [The J. M. Smucker Company Announces Executive Changes | MarketScreener](https://www.marketscreener.com/news/the-j-m-smucker-company-announces-executive-changes-ce7e5ad8d980f521). Effective July 27, 2026, Robert Crane will become SVP, Head of Sales and International, and Timothy Wayne will become SVP and GM, Coffee and Away From Home [The J. M. Smucker Company (SJM) Announces Key Executive Changes](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8602566/the-j-m-smucker-company-sjm-announces-key-executive-changes).
*   **Board Activism:** Following engagement with Elliott Investment Management, Woo-Sung (Bruce) Chung and David Singer were appointed to the Board of Directors, effective April 15, 2026 [The J.M. Smucker Co. Appoints Two New Independent Directors](https://investors.jmsmucker.com/news/news-details/2026/The-J-M--Smucker-Co--Appoints-Two-New-Independent-Directors/default.aspx).

**Strategic Initiatives & Portfolio Shifts**
J.M. Smucker is aggressively reshaping its portfolio to focus on high-growth categories while divesting non-core assets:
*   **Uncrustables Expansion:** The company is heavily investing in Uncrustables, targeting it as a "$1 billion-plus brand platform" [J.M. Smucker Strategy and Business Model](https://umbrex.com/resources/company-profiles/j-m-smucker/).
*   **Hostess Integration:** Following its November 2023 acquisition, Hostess Brands is being integrated into a new 'Sweet Baked Snacks' segment. The company is targeting $100 million in cost synergies by the end of fiscal 2026 and has developed specific plans to return the brand to growth [J.M. Smucker Strategy and Business Model](https://umbrex.com/resources/company-profiles/j-m-smucker/); [The J.M. Smucker Co. Highlights Transformed Portfolio and World-Class Capabilities at 2024 Investor Day](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/the-jm-smucker-co-highlights-transformed-portfolio-and-world-class-capabilities-at-2024-investor-day-302327118.html).
*   **Divestitures:** The company closed the divestiture of the Voortman brand on December 2, 2024, and sold certain Sweet Baked Snacks value brands on March 3, 2025 [The J.M. Smucker Co. Announces Fiscal Year 2026 Fourth Quarter Results and Provides Full-Year Fiscal 2027 Outlook](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/the-jm-smucker-co-announces-fiscal-year-2026-fourth-quarter-results-and-provides-full-year-fiscal-2027-outlook-302795140.html).
*   **Category Focus:** Strategic priorities include unlocking premium and cold coffee formats, accelerating dog snack and cat food sales, and expanding margins in international markets [The J.M. Smucker Co. Highlights Transformed Portfolio and World-Class Capabilities at 2024 Investor Day](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/the-jm-smucker-co-highlights-transformed-portfolio-and-world-class-capabilities-at-2024-investor-day-302327118.html). However, the Milk Bone brand is facing pressure from cautious consumer spending and is not expected to return to growth until the second half of fiscal 2026 [Smucker (SJM) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript @themotleyfool #stocks $SJM](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2025/08/27/smucker-sjm-q1-2026-earnings-call-transcript/).

**Production, Manufacturing Scale & Capacity Changes**
As of February 2026, the company operates 21 manufacturing and supply chain facilities across the U.S., having recently split its supply chain and manufacturing divisions into separate organizations [JM Smucker splits supply chain, manufacturing functions | Supply Chain Dive](https://www.supplychaindive.com/news/smuckers-supply-chain-executive-bryan-hutson-randy-day/742736/); [JM Smucker eliminates COO as part of leadership restructuring | Food Dive](https://www.fooddive.com/news/jm-smucker-eliminates-coo-as-part-of-leadership-restructuring/811808/). Recent capacity changes include:
*   **McCalla, Alabama:** Opened a new 900,000-square-foot Uncrustables facility and distribution center ($1.1 billion investment, 750 jobs) in November 2024, followed by an additional $27 million expansion announced in January 2026 [Smucker's $800M Uncrustables Brand Eyes $1B Sales With New Mega-Facility | SJM Stock News](https://www.stocktitan.net/news/SJM/the-j-m-smucker-co-celebrates-opening-of-the-newest-uncrustables-yxy9xsi3arpx.html); [Smucker Expands Alabama Manufacturing Footprint with $27M Investment in McCalla Facility - Expansion Solutions](https://www.expansionsolutionsmagazine.com/smucker-expands-alabama-manufacturing-facility/).
*   **Hostess Realignment:** Investing over $120 million to expand the Hostess plant in Columbus, Georgia, while planning to close and sell the Hostess plant in Indianapolis by early 2026 [JM Smucker to spend over $120M on Hostess plant expansion | Food Dive](https://www.fooddive.com/news/jm-smucker-spending-more-than-120m-to-expand-hostess-plant/801792/); [JM Smucker to spend over $120M on Hostess plant expansion | Supply Chain Dive](https://www.supplychaindive.com/news/jm-smucker-spending-more-than-120m-to-expand-hostess-plant/802595/).
*   **Other Expansions:** Completed a 240,000-square-foot expansion at the Longmont, Colorado Uncrustables plant; built a 29,000-square-foot R&D center in Orrville, Ohio; invested $52.6 million in Buffalo, New York for Milk-Bone production; and invested $20.5 million in Topeka, Kansas for pet food [J.M. Smucker to build R&D center for Uncrustables | Food Dive](https://www.fooddive.com/news/smucker-uncrustables-rd-center-ohio/637988/); [J.M. Smucker to invest $52M to upgrade New York production facility](https://www.petfoodindustry.com/news-newsletters/pet-food-news/news/15741250/jm-smucker-to-invest-52m-to-upgrade-new-york-production-facility).

**Gaps and Limitations**
Based on the provided research findings, the following requested information could not be verified:
*   **Promotional Effectiveness:** No specific data or commentary was available regarding the ROI or effectiveness of recent promotional campaigns.
*   **Distribution Network Expansion:** Aside from the new distribution center attached to the McCalla, Alabama facility, there are no specific details on broader distribution network changes or logistics partnerships.
*   **Ineffective Cost-Cutting:** While the company is targeting $100 million in Hostess synergies, there is no data indicating recent cost-cutting initiatives that executed on plan but failed to move bottom-line results.
